project_description: Complete overhaul of ForgeOS — an AI-powered web app builder with a cockpit UI, workspace runner, and GitHub-to-Render deploy chain — replacing a broken multi-agent pipeline with a single Claude-powered builder, persistent memory brain, and clean agent boundaries.

project_description.session_title: Dead Code Cleanup, Bug Fixes, and VS Code Workflow Migration
shipped: Removed self-require from server/pipeline/agents.js — line 12 had agents.js requiring itself, causing circular dependency warnings on startup

Fixed subdomain proxy body forwarding in server/index.js — changed body: req to body: JSON.stringify(req.body) and added content-type header so POST requests through *.forge-os.ai subdomains correctly forward JSON bodies
Fixed NEON_DATABASE_URL vs DATABASE_URL mismatch — published apps must use process.env.NEON_DATABASE_URL, not process.env.DATABASE_URL
Fixed Render API createService plan — changed plan: 'starter' to plan: 'standard' in render-api.js so all newly published apps deploy always-on
Wired assets context injection into builder.js — added assetsManager.getAssetsContext(projectId) call alongside Brain context, injected into systemPrompt before build
Identified CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS missing from agents.js — deleted in prior dead code scrub, Chat Agent was running with undefined system prompt
Restored all prompt constants to agents.js — FORGE_VOICE, EXECUTOR_INSTRUCTIONS, EXECUTOR_ITERATE_INSTRUCTIONS, EXECUTOR_FIX_INSTRUCTIONS, AUDITOR_INSTRUCTIONS, CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS
Migrated dev workflow from Replit to VS Code + GitHub — changes pushed directly from VS Code, Render auto-deploys from main branch, Replit sunset
decisions: Replit is sunset as the dev environment — VS Code is now the sole development environment
All changes pushed directly from VS Code to GitHub, Render auto-deploys from main
Claude can push directly to GitHub repo via PAT when provided — eliminates manual file copy workflow
Assets are project-scoped — generated apps must fetch assets via /api/projects/PROJECT_ID/assets/FILENAME
PROJECT_ID should be injected as an env var per project so generated apps can reference assets without hardcoded IDs — systemic fix pending
Subdomain proxy must serialize req.body as JSON string, not pipe req stream directly — express.json() consumes the stream before proxy runs
pending: Add PROJECT_ID to per-project env vars injected at runtime so generated apps can reference assets portably
Update builder prompt to document AVAILABLE PROJECT ASSETS pattern and PROJECT_ID env var usage
Verify assets context injection end-to-end with a real build using an uploaded CSV
Revoke and rotate GitHub PAT used in this session

shipped: Fixed 'Invalid Date' bug — published_at BIGINT from Neon returned as string, fixed with Number(row.published_at)
Fixed 'Workspace files not found' on restore — rebuildWorkspaceFromSnapshot now checks both executor.output.files and builder.output.files
Fixed Global Secrets not passing to published app child processes — getMergedEnv now calls getSecretsAsObject()
Migrated ForgeOS to Render persistent disk at /data — all workspace and published app paths now use DATA_DIR env var
Fixed all hardcoded __dirname-relative data paths across workspace/manager.js, publish/manager.js, projects/manager.js, runtime-backup.js, pipeline/runner.js, builder.js, index.js
Built email auth gate — server/auth/gate.js with HMAC-signed cookie session and ALLOWED_EMAILS whitelist
Built Render API wrapper — server/publish/render-api.js with createService, updateServiceEnv, redeployService, getServiceStatus, deleteService, listServices
Built pushToAppBranch in server/publish/github.js — pushes app files to dedicated apps/<slug> branch with standalone empty parent commit
Added render_service_id and render_url columns to published_apps table
decisions: Published apps deploy as independent Render Web Services, not child processes
Each app gets its own branch apps/<slug> in BrianBMorgan/ForgeOS repo
Render auto-deploys from that branch on push
DATA_DIR=/data on Render for all persistent data
ALLOWED_EMAILS env var controls ForgeOS access
Global Secrets Vault passes secrets to published app Render services via updateServiceEnv
pending: Rewrite publishProject() to use Render API — carried to March 8

session_log.session_title: Render Publish Pipeline, Wildcard Subdomains, Assets
shipped: Rewrote _doPublish to use Render API — pushes to GitHub branch, creates/redeploys Render service, passes env vars
Fixed render-api.js exports — pushProjectToGitHub was incorrectly included
Fixed envSpecificDetails requirement in Render API createService call
Fixed getSecretsAsObject lazy DB init — secrets now pass automatically to every published Render service
Fixed handle existing Render service on republish — graceful fallback when service name already in use
Replaced child-process restore with Render service restore in _restoreOne
Added renderUrl to getPublishedApp response and Publish tab UI

Major dead code scrub of publish/manager.js — removed spawn, port management, runInstall, runBuild, copyDirectory, patchHardcodedPort, attachLogs, killProcess, stopPublishedApp, getNextFreePort, validateCommand, resolveStartCommand, buildProcessEnv, collectJsFiles, ensurePublishedDir
Wildcard *.forge-os.ai DNS and TLS via Namecheap + Render — all three CNAME records added
Built wildcard subdomain proxy middleware in index.js — routes *.forge-os.ai to correct Render service
Fixed content encoding bug in subdomain proxy — accept-encoding: identity prevents ERR_CONTENT_DECODING_FAILED
Updated Publish tab UI to show subdomain URL as primary public URL
Added slug editor to Publish tab — editable field with save, preview, and warning
Built renameSlug in publish/manager.js — creates new Render service, deletes old one, updates DB
Stage 1 tech stack unbans — react, dotenv, jsonwebtoken, svelte removed from banned list in agents.js
Built project_assets table in Neon
Built server/assets/manager.js — saveAsset, getAsset, listAssets, deleteAsset, getAssetsContext
Built assets API routes in index.js — GET/POST/DELETE with busboy multipart handling
Built AssetsTab UI component with drag-and-drop upload, file list, Copy URL, Delete
Injected AVAILABLE PROJECT ASSETS block into builder prompt via runner.js
decisions: Assets stored in Neon DB as base64 (binary) or UTF-8 text (CSV/JSON/text)
Asset access URL pattern: /api/projects/:id/assets/:filename
Wildcard subdomain proxy uses fetch with accept-encoding: identity to avoid compression issues
Slug rename creates new Render service and deletes old — no in-place rename
BASE_DOMAIN=forge-os.ai env var controls subdomain proxy domain
Stage 2 unbans (React via npm, Vue, Next, Nuxt) deferred — CDN usage already works
Custom BYO domains deferred until actually needed
Session log protocol established — appended to Forge_additional_context_pack.json at end of each session
pending: Verify assets context injection works end-to-end with a real build
Stage 2 tech stack unbans when needed
Custom domain support (BYO domain with CNAME generation via Render API)
ForgeOS Brain — wire forge_workspace_builder_prompt into single /api/projects/:id/build endpoint
pgvector semantic search upgrade for Brain once sufficient memory entries exist
Clean up old /apps/:slug child-process proxy handler in index.js — now dead code
open_questions: Should failed builds auto-record to Brain or require human confirmation?
What is the right conversation history limit per project before summarization is needed?
Should workspace builder stream responses or return complete JSON on completion?

domain_knowledge: ForgeOS serves workspace previews at /preview/:runId/ and published apps at /apps/:slug/ behind a path-prefix proxy that rewrites HTML href/src/action attributes and patches window.fetch(), XMLHttpRequest.open(), and history.pushState at runtime
All Claude-generated workspace apps must use root-relative URLs with a leading slash (e.g. /api/items, /style.css) — the proxy handles prefix rewriting, apps must never do it themselves
Never add <base> tags to any app — they break proxy rewriting
The fetch URL audit rule was inverted in the original Auditor prompt — correct rule is: fetch argument must start with / and must not contain ://
ForgeOS apps run with node server.js — no build steps, no bundlers, no chained commands, CommonJS only
The platform injects environment variables at runtime — no dotenv, no .env files
PORT must always be process.env.PORT || 3000 — never hardcoded
Every app requires GET / returning a complete HTML page — not a redirect, not JSON
Database: @neondatabase/serverless only, all CREATE TABLE statements use IF NOT EXISTS, FK column types must exactly match referenced PK types
Authentication: Neon Auth via jose + JWKS only — no bcrypt, no custom JWT signing, no passport
Claude frequently wraps JSON responses in markdown fences — always strip before JSON.parse using: text.replace(/^```(?:json)?\s*/m, '').replace(/\s*```$/m, '') then scan for first { or [
model-router.js in ForgeOS already handles fence stripping — all new Anthropic API calls must route through it, never call the API directly from route handlers
publish/manager.js uses a reservedPorts Set to prevent concurrent port allocation races — ports are reserved immediately on getNextFreePort() and released only after process exit
stopPublishedApp awaits full process exit (SIGTERM then SIGKILL after grace period) before filesystem operations — this prevents file handle conflicts on republish
Restore after redeploy: files are rebuilt from DB snapshot AND npm install runs — node_modules do not survive redeploy
Env var merge order in published apps: platform keys → global defaults → project-specific vars. process.env is never spread wholesale into child processes
copyDirectory skips both node_modules and .git — no git history in published apps
exportProject uses execFileSync with args array, not execSync with shell string — eliminates shell injection risk
The multi-agent pipeline (Planner → Reviewer → Executor → Auditor) has been replaced with a single Claude claude-opus-4-5 call using the workspace builder prompt
Replit Agent builds ForgeOS itself (cockpit, server/index.js, pipeline code) — Claude builds workspace apps. These are separate domains that must never be confused

ForgeOS Brain (server/memory/brain.js) provides persistent memory across all projects and team members via Neon Postgres tables: forge_memory, forge_project_index, forge_team_prefs, forge_conversations
Brain memory is non-blocking — extractMemory is called after successful builds but never blocks the build response
CSS url() references must also use root-relative paths (url('/images/bg.png')) — the proxy HTML rewriter does not touch stylesheets
The Chat Agent degradation pattern: after failed iterations it loses confidence and reaches for logging/try-catch — this is a named failure mode, not acceptable behavior
Logging is never a fix. try-catch is never a fix. Find the actual broken code.
buildSuggestion must contain: file + route/function + current broken code + replacement. One sentence. No outcome descriptions.

GPT context packs should be consolidated to one JSON per project before import, then split on ingestion: domain_knowledge/decisions/terminology → project instructions, patterns/mistakes/preferences → ForgeOS Brain

decisions_made.decision: Replace 11-prompt multi-agent pipeline with single Claude workspace builder prompt
decisions_made.rationale: Pipeline had too many inter-agent communication failure points, required constant prompt maintenance, and still produced inconsistent first-pass output. Single model with full context reasons through the whole build in one pass and iterates conversationally.

decisions_made.decision: Drop JSON wrapper from Chat Agent output
decisions_made.rationale: JSON structure caused message and buildSuggestion to be treated as separate concerns, leading to contradictions between the two fields. Plain text with appended BUILD: line is simpler and harder to get wrong.

decisions_made.decision: Move response format and banned formats to bottom of Chat Agent prompt
decisions_made.rationale: Under context pressure models comply with what they read last. Format rules at the bottom are followed more consistently than format rules at the top.

decisions_made.decision: All Anthropic API calls use claude-opus-4-5
decisions_made.rationale: Most capable model, chosen for quality over cost given the stakes of first-pass build quality.

decisions_made.decision: Memory extraction uses claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 not opus
decisions_made.rationale: Memory extraction is a lightweight classification task — haiku is fast and cheap, opus is unnecessary overhead for this use case.

decisions_made.decision: ForgeOS Brain stores memories in Neon Postgres, not a separate vector DB
decisions_made.rationale: Neon supports pgvector natively — no additional infrastructure needed. Falls back to recency/usefulness ordering if pgvector is not available.

decisions_made.decision: Auditor planDeviationDetected flag added to response schema
decisions_made.rationale: Plan deviation (Executor built something not in the approved plan) is categorically different from implementation bugs. It requires reverting to the plan, not patching the deviation. The flag signals the Fix pass to take a different action.

decisions_made.decision: publish/manager.js uses awaited process teardown before filesystem operations
decisions_made.rationale: Original setTimeout-based SIGKILL was fire-and-forget, causing file handle conflicts when republishing. Full await ensures clean teardown.

decisions_made.decision: Replit Agent gets a dedicated system prompt establishing domain boundaries
decisions_made.rationale: Without explicit boundaries Replit Agent attempts to generate workspace app code itself, conflicting with Claude's role. The prompt establishes: Replit builds the factory, Claude runs the machines.

preferences.value: Never use 'refactor' — the correct term is 'reforge'
Plain HTML/CSS/JS — no frameworks, no build steps
claude-opus-4-5 for all primary builds and reasoning tasks
Conversational — expect iteration, build on what works, don't rebuild from scratch
Direct, no hedging, no try-catch suggestions, no logging suggestions
Docx files for reference documents, .md for prompts and integration guides

patterns_that_worked: Single Claude call with full platform constraints replaces multi-agent pipeline — simpler, fewer failure points
Memory extraction as a non-blocking post-build call — never blocks user-facing response
reservedPorts Set pattern for port allocation — prevents concurrent allocation races without distributed locking
Awaited process teardown (SIGTERM → SIGKILL with grace period) before filesystem operations
buildContext() prepended to system prompt as a separate block — clean separation of memory from instructions
Per-project conversation history stored in DB and passed as messages array — gives Claude full build journey awareness
Pre-emit self-checklist in Executor prompt — model gates itself before output rather than relying solely on Auditor
Fence-strip + first-brace-scan pattern for all Claude JSON responses — handles all three Claude output styles
Consolidate GPT context packs to one JSON per project before import, split on ingestion by type
mistakes_to_avoid: Fetch URL audit rule inverted — 'no absolute paths starting with /' banned path-relative URLs instead of absolute ones
Chat Agent suggesting logging or try-catch after failed iterations — named as degradation pattern, explicitly banned
Executor inventing mechanisms not in the approved plan (bootstrap.js circular auth dependency) — plan deviation requires revert not patch
Spreading process.env wholesale into child processes — host secrets bleed into published apps, project vars lose precedence
Using setTimeout for process teardown without awaiting — causes file handle conflicts on republish
Storing only stage pass/fail status without raw payloads — agent cannot diagnose parse failures without the actual string
Running npm install in restore path only when files are missing — node_modules don't survive redeploy even when files do
Copying .git directory into published app files
Using execSync with shell string interpolation for paths — use execFileSync with args array
JSON wrapper on Chat Agent output causing message/buildSuggestion contradictions
Format rules at top of long prompts — model drops them under context pressure
URL helper functions (apiUrl(), buildUrl()) that construct absolute URLs — fix fetch() calls directly with /path strings
Multiple code changes in a single buildSuggestion — one root cause, one code change
current_state: ForgeOS pipeline has been completely overhauled. The 11-prompt multi-agent pipeline (Planner → Reviewer → Executor → Auditor) has been replaced with a single Claude workspace builder prompt (forge_workspace_builder_prompt.md). The Chat Agent has been reforged with explicit degradation prevention and plain text output. The Replit Agent has a system prompt establishing clear domain boundaries. publish/manager.js has been reforged with port reservation, awaited process teardown, restore-path npm install, and correct env var merge order. The ForgeOS Brain module (server/memory/brain.js) is ready to drop in — provides persistent memory across all projects and team members via Neon Postgres. All reference documents have been exported as .docx and .md files. The deploy chain (Replit → GitHub → Render → production) remains intact.
next_steps: Wire forge_workspace_builder_prompt.md into a single /api/projects/:id/build endpoint replacing the pipeline runner
Drop brain.js into server/memory/brain.js and wire into build endpoint per brain_integration.md
Add Brain tab to ForgeOS cockpit UI
Add /api/brain/import endpoint for GPT context pack ingestion
Test first build through the simplified single-prompt pipeline
Import GPT context packs into ForgeOS Brain via the import endpoint
Consider pgvector semantic search upgrade once Brain has sufficient memory entries
open_questions: Should the workspace builder stream responses to the cockpit or return complete JSON on completion?
What is the right conversation history limit per project before summarization is needed?
Should failed builds auto-record to Brain or require human confirmation that it was genuinely a mistake?
pgvector availability on current Neon instance — needs verification before relying on semantic search

key_terminology.value: The correct term for what other systems call 'refactor' — we reforge code, never refactor it
The AI-powered web app builder cockpit built in Replit, published externally via Render
A directory containing files for a single generated app, identified by runId
The persistent memory module (server/memory/brain.js) that stores patterns, mistakes, preferences and project history across all ForgeOS builds
The ForgeOS UI — the tab strip (Plan/Review/Diff/Auditor/Render/Shell/DB/Env/Publish) and preview environment
server/publish/manager.js — handles the full lifecycle of making a workspace app publicly accessible
The ForgeOS middleware that serves apps at /preview/:runId/ and /apps/:slug/ and rewrites HTML URLs to stay within the proxy path
A structured JSON export of project knowledge in a model-agnostic format for portability between AI systems
The Chat Agent failure mode where after failed iterations it loses confidence and suggests logging or try-catch instead of finding the actual broken code
When the Executor builds something not specified in the approved plan — requires reverting to the plan, not patching the deviation
Removing markdown code fences (```json ... ```) from Claude API responses before JSON.parse — required because Claude often wraps JSON in fences
The ForgeOS settings feature that auto-injects API keys and secrets as environment variables at runtime
